title: graffenrieda micrantha
intro: Graffenrieda micrantha, also known as the "Balsam of the Andes," is a plant native to the Andean region in South America. Its medicinal properties lie in its ability to aid in digestion, reduce inflammation, and alleviate respiratory issues due to its antiseptic and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Graffenrieda micrantha include relief from coughs, colds, and sore throats, as well as its use as a natural remedy for digestive issues such as constipation and diarrhea. Herbal preparations of Graffenrieda micrantha are typically made by infusing the leaves in hot water to create a tea, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
